        <Breakfast>
          <Meal>

              <item name="milk" />
              <item name="banana" />
              <item name="almonds" />

          <Meal>

            <short_description>Creamy overnight oats with apple and cinnamon—a fiber- and protein-rich, mildly spiced breakfast.</short_description>

              <item name="milk" />
              <item name="plain yogurt" />
              <item name="apple" />
              <item name="cinnamon" />

            <nutrition_note>Balanced mix of fiber (oats, apple), protein and calcium (milk and yogurt); cinnamon adds flavor with minimal sugar. To lower sugar, use unsweetened dairy or dairy-free alternatives and skip any additional sweeteners.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Whole-Grain Pancakes — Lemon-Herb Skillet</name>
            <short_description>Whole-grain pancakes with eggs and milk, topped with berries and a light maple sweetness.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Pair with a side of yogurt or extra berries to boost protein and fiber; limit maple syrup to 1–2 tsp or swap for a berry compote; add lemon zest or fresh herbs to echo the lemon-herb concept without adding sugar.</meal_suggestion>

              <item name="berries" />

            <meal_suggestion>Use whole-grain pasta for extra fiber, and if available add more vegetables (e.g., spinach, peppers) and a squeeze of lemon for brightness. Keep olive oil portions moderate to manage calories.</meal_suggestion>

            <name>Roast Chicken Dinner — Smoky Plate</name>
            <short_description>Smoky roast chicken thighs served with quinoa and roasted broccoli and carrots—a protein- and fiber-rich dinner.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Aim for a balanced plate: half vegetables (broccoli and carrots), one-quarter protein (chicken), one-quarter quinoa. For leaner fat, use skinless thighs; boost flavor with smoked paprika or chipotle. Drizzle a small amount of olive oil during roasting for healthy fats.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>

Moderate fat from thighs; skinless options reduce fat. Gluten-free and naturally balanced for a dinner.</nutrition_note>
          </Meal>
          <Meal>
            <name>Sheet-Pan Pork Dinner — Berry Boost Skillet</name>
            <short_description>One-pan sheet-pan pork tenderloin with potatoes and green beans, seasoned with olive oil and herbs.</short_description>
            <meal_suggestion>Roast on a sheet pan: toss pork tenderloin (whole or sliced into medallions), potato chunks, and trimmed green beans with olive oil, salt, pepper, and herbs (rosemary or thyme). Arrange on a single pan and roast at 425°F (220°C) for 25–30 minutes, until the pork reaches 145–150°F (63–66°C) and potatoes are tender. Rest briefly, then slice pork and serve with the vegetables.</meal_suggestion>
            <ingredients>
              <item name="pork tenderloin" />
              <item name="potatoes" />
              <item name="green beans" />
              <item name="olive oil" />
            </ingredients>
            <nutrition_note>Lean pork tenderloin offers high-quality protein; potatoes provide energy-rich complex carbohydrates; green beans contribute fiber and micronutrients; olive oil supplies heart-healthy fats. This one-pan approach supports balanced portions and easier cleanup; adjust portions to fit dietary needs.</nutrition_note>
